title: SRS Relation Type Definition
description: Declares a named relation type within a package's relation type vocabulary. Relation types describe the semantic meaning and structural shape of a class of relations.

id.description: Stable UUID identity for this relation type definition.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version number. Starts at 1.

key.description: The string stored in Relation.relationType — a canonical bare string (e.g. `supersedes`) or custom `namespace/name` string (e.g. `org.example/my_type`). Unified key field per RFC-006 VocabularyEntry substrate. Must be globally unique across the effective installed package set.

namespace.description: The package namespace this relation type belongs to.

label.description: Short human-readable label for display in views and tooling.

description.description: Full description of the semantic meaning of this relation type.

canonicalDirection.description: Human-readable description of which end is source and which is target, or what members represent.

inverseType.description: The relationType of the inverse relation, if one is formally declared.

irreflexive.description: When true, a relation from an instance to itself is invalid.

allowedSourceTypes.description: Allowed semanticObjectType values for the source instance. Absent means any type is permitted.

allowedTargetTypes.description: Allowed semanticObjectType values for the target instance. Absent means any type is permitted.

requireSameSemanticObjectType.description: When true, source and target must have the same semanticObjectType.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Absent means active. deprecated: resolves but new writes rejected. tombstone: resolves for reads only. retired: does not resolve.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this definition was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this definition was last updated.

properties.description: Arbitrary metadata per substrate Change H policy. Unknown top-level fields are rejected; use this instead.
